coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy Z2 recovery, 45–55 min on relatively flat ground. Your legs took a pounding this weekend—respect that. Aim for 130–145 HR (adjust for travel lag if you're between time zones). This isn't a fitness day; it's active recovery that clears metabolic waste and preps you for Tuesday's threshold work. If you feel stiff or sluggish in the first 10 min, that's normal. Don't chase pace.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You're in peak training load on a Monday travel day—a worst-case fuel scenario. Eat 1.2–1.5g carbs per kg bodyweight today (split across 4–5 meals if meals are irregular). Prioritize carbs in the 2 hours post-run to start glycogen replenishment. If you're on a plane or in transit, pack real food: rice cakes, bananas, energy bars. Hydration matters more than you think at altitude preparation; aim for pale urine by afternoon.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Prioritize 8+ hours of sleep tonight despite jet lag or work stress. Your CNS is fatigued in week 19 of peak, and Tuesday's threshold workout demands a rested nervous system. If you're traveling, use blackout curtains and consider a 20 min nap this afternoon if possible. Cold plunge or ice bath for 8–10 min post-easy run accelerates recovery; skip if it disrupts sleep.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 109 days out and in the sharpest 4-week block of the cycle. The Julian Alps demand precision on steep descents and mental resilience at 40K+. Use today's easy run to visualize the Kranjska Gora course—specifically the descent tech sections. You've built the aerobic engine; now start building confidence in your ability to execute under fatigue. Treat Tuesday's threshold work as a direct simulation of race pace decision-making.
